site.title: Gemar Belajar
site.subtitle: Interactive Learning Visualizations
site.tagline: Learn science & math the fun way!

home.heroDesc: Explore science and mathematics through interactive 3D visualizations. Designed for Indonesian students from elementary to high school.
home.tataSuryaCard: Explore the planets in our solar system. See how they orbit the sun!
home.gravitasiCard: Understand Newton's law of gravity through interactive simulations. Learn about orbits and gravitational forces.
home.gelombangCard: Visualize sinusoidal waves. Adjust amplitude, frequency, and phase in real-time!
home.matematikaCard: Plot mathematical function graphs. See how graphs change as coefficients are adjusted.

tataSurya.title: 3D Solar System
tataSurya.description: Explore the planets in our solar system. Drag to rotate, scroll to zoom, click a planet for detailed info!

info.name: Name
info.clickHint: Click a planet to see information

funFacts.matahari: The Sun is the closest star to Earth. Its diameter is 109 times Earth's diameter!
funFacts.merkurius: Mercury is the smallest planet and closest to the Sun. A year on Mercury is only 88 Earth days!
funFacts.venus: Venus is the hottest planet even though it's not the closest to the Sun. Its temperature can reach 465°C!
funFacts.bumi: Earth is the only known planet with life. About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water!
funFacts.mars: Mars is called the 'Red Planet' because its surface is rich in iron oxide (rust)!
funFacts.jupiter: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. Its mass is 2.5 times all other planets combined!
funFacts.saturnus: Saturn's rings consist of billions of ice and rock particles. The rings span up to 282,000 km!
funFacts.uranus: Uranus rotates with an 98° tilted axis, so it 'rolls' around the Sun!
funFacts.neptunus: Neptune has the fastest winds in the solar system, reaching 2,100 km/h!

footer.tentang: Gemar Belajar is an interactive educational visualization platform to help Indonesian students understand science and math concepts.
